page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is provided via a bundled 1843 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48ds_v4 demonstrates balanced performance characteristics. Its memory subsystem is a notable highlight, achieving a strong rating in the Passmark memory score, supported by a read-only small-block cached memory bandwidth of 2495.44 GB/sec and a large-block uncached read bandwidth of 104.44 GB/sec. CPU-bound tasks fall within the average range, with a Passmark CPU score of 35712.26 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 44208.53. The server handles database operations effectively, registering 15582.99 on Passmark database tests and processing 34.77 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

While the server excels in memory-intensive operations and general-purpose workloads, it exhibits performance limitations in complex machine learning tasks. LLM inference benchmarks reveal weak text generation speeds, yielding 50.02 tokens per second on a small 135M model and dropping to 7.75 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Consequently, this instance is not optimal for large-scale AI generation tasks. However, its balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio, high-capacity local storage, and strong memory performance make it highly cost-efficient for enterprise databases, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose business applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It offers 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance includes a 1843 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and a small-block cached read bandwidth of 2495.44 GB/sec. CPU and database operations perform in the average tier, while LLM text generation benchmarks indicate weak performance. This server is highly suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads where balanced compute, memory, and local storage are required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 1843 GB local disk, delivering strong memory performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 1843 GB local disk for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and strong memory performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Bundled 1843 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities
Strong memory performance with high-bandwidth cached read speeds
Well-suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
Weak performance on large language model text generation tasks
